description: The ultimate set-and-forget houseplant. Survives 2-3 weeks without water, grows into a miniature tree over the years, and looks good on any windowsill. Perfect first succulent.

care.water_frequency: Every 2-3 weeks, let soil dry completely between waterings
care.light_preferred: Bright indirect to direct sun (south or west window)
care.light_also_ok: Medium light (survives but grows leggy and slow)
care.humidity_action: No misting needed. Normal apartment air is perfect.
care.soil_types: Well-draining succulent mix with coarse sand or perlite. Terracotta pot helps soil dry faster.
care.repot_frequency: Every 2-3 years. Prefers a snug pot.
care.fertilizer_type: Diluted balanced fertilizer 2-3 times during spring and summer
care.difficulty: Easy — forgives missed watering, only dies from overwatering or frost
care.watering_guide: Stick your finger 3 cm into the soil. If dry, water thoroughly until it drains from the bottom. If even slightly damp, wait another few days.
care.light_guide: Place within 1 meter of a south or west-facing window for best results. Can tolerate a few hours of direct sun. Moves away from the window = leggier growth.
care.tips: Overwatering is the #1 killer. When in doubt, wait another week. Develops a thick woody trunk with age — can become a stunning bonsai-like showpiece over 5-10 years.
care.toxicity_note: Toxic to cats and dogs if ingested — causes vomiting and lethargy. Keep on a high shelf if you have pets.
common_problems: Soft, mushy leaves (overwatering — let it dry out and reduce frequency)
Leggy, stretched growth (needs more light — move closer to window)
common_pests: Mealybugs (white cottony spots — wipe with rubbing alcohol)
Spider mites (tiny webs — increase air circulation)
Scale (brown bumps on stems — scrape off manually)
